leadText: A Yache Berry (Japanese: ヤチェのみ Yache Fruit) is a type of Berry introduced in Generation IV.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Held item 1.2 Ingredient 1.3 Exchange 1.3.1 Pokémon Dream World Effect [ edit | edit source ] Held item [ edit | edit source ] If the holder is hit by a supereffective Ice-type attack, it will consume its held Yache Berry and halve the attack's damage. If the holder has Ripen , the damage is multiplied by 1/4. Ingredient [ edit | edit source ] Berry Blending OR AS Yache Berries are blue Berries that have a medium chance of producing a Pokéblock +. Poffin Cooking D P Pt BD SP When used alone, Yache Berries will produce Poffins with Sourness 13, Dryness 8 and smoothness 29 when cooked in 1 minute with no spills or burns. Ice Cream Scoop DW Flavor 1 Flavor 2 Flavor 3 Easy to scoop Stickiness Juice Shoppe X Y Yache Berries are blue Berries in Group 2. Composting X Y Yache Berries are blue Berries. Dye Houses S M US UM 30 Yache Berries, Passho Berries , and Coba Berries are used to dye any white or dyed clothing into dark blue. Curry Sw Sh Yache Berries have a rarity of 5. Cram-o-matic Sw Sh Yache Berries are Ice-type items and give 4 points towards the output value. Exchange [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Dream World [ edit | edit source ] In the Pokémon Dream World , the player could exchange Berries for Décor . The following Décor required Yache Berries. Catalogue Décor Price Pokémon Doll Catalogue Salamence Doll 15 Yache Berries

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] Yache Berries raise Snorlax's Strength, doubling the effect if the Berry is Snorlax's favorite. The base amount of Strength raised by the Berry is determined by the level of the helper Pokémon that gathered it.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description D P Pt A Poffin ingredient. If held by a Pokémon, it weakens a foe's supereffective Ice-type attack. HG SS If held by a Pokémon, it weakens a foe's supereffective Ice-type attack. B W B2 W2 Weakens a supereffective Ice-type attack against the holding Pokémon. X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA If held by a Pokémon, this Berry will lessen the damage taken from one supereffective Ice-type attack. S V If a Pokémon holding this Berry is hit with a supereffective Ice-type move, the power of that move will be weakened. Tag information [ edit | edit source ] No. 40 Size: 5.3" (13.5 cm) Firmness: Very hard Games Description D P Pt BD SP This Berry has a refreshing flavor that strikes a good balance of dryness and sourness. It tastes better chilled. Curry information [ edit | edit source ] Games Description Sw Sh A refreshing Berry with dry and sour flavors. Eating a chilled Yache Berry cools your heated body.

B W Castelia City (gift from Iris ) Held by wild Starly (5% chance) W B2 W2 Join Avenue ( Flower Shop ) DW * First completion of the tutorial (player will be given five of one of the damage-reducing Berries from Fennel ) X Y Route 19 , Inverse House Blue Berry trees OR AS Route 123 Inverse Battle Stop Master Rank Pokémon Contest Spectacular (given by fan) PMC * Poké Doll Grabber (Lv. 1) S M US UM Berry piles ( Poni Wilds , Poni Plains ) Sw Sh Berry trees ( Lake of Outrage ) Sw Sh IA Berry trees ( Fields of Honor ) Sw Sh CT Berry trees ( Frostpoint Field ) BD SP Pastoria City Picked up by walking Pokémon (Hardy, Brave, Adamant, Naughty, Bold, Impish, Hasty, Sassy, Quirky Natures ) S V Sparkling overworld item ( Socarrat Trail ) Distribution [ edit | edit source ] Games Event Language/Region Distribution period Sw Sh Pokémon Center Curry Ingredients and Berries Japan November 15, 2019 to October 31, 2021 As a held item [ edit | edit source ] Games Event Language/Region Distribution period D P Strongest Dragonite Japanese January 15 to February 14, 2008 Toys "R" Us Dragonite United States, Puerto Rico November 8 to 9, 2008 B W B2 W2 Pokémon Adventure Camp Fossil Archen Japanese July 21, 2012 to September 30, 2013 Growth [ edit | edit source ] Generation IV Planted 0 hours Sprouted 18 hours Taller 36 hours Flowering 54 hours Berries 72 hours A Yache plant will yield 1-5 Berries. A growing Yache plant's soil dries by 6% every hour, fully drying out in 17 hours. Generation V Planted 0 hours Sprouted 27 hours Taller 54 hours Flowering 81 hours Berries 108 hours A Yache plant will yield ? Berries. Generation VI Pokémon X and Y Planted 0 hours Sprouted 8 hours Growing 16 hours Bigger 24 hours Budding 32 hours Bloom 40 hours Berries 48 hours A Yache plant will yield 3-20 Berries. During its growth, watering the plant adds 1 Berries to the final harvest, weeding it adds 0.5, and removing a pest adds 2. Yache plants attract Volbeat as pests. Pokémon Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire Planted 0 hours Sprouted 8 hours Taller 16 hours Flowering 24 hours Berries 32 hours A Yache plant will yield 2-10 Berries. Generation VII Pokémon Sun, Moon, Ultra Sun, and Ultra Moon Planted 0 hours Sprouted 30 hours Taller 36 hours Flowering 44 hours Berries 48 hours A Yache plant will yield 2 Berries if Isle Aplenny is at Phase 1, 4 Berries if it is at Phase 2, and 6 Berries if it is at Phase 3. Generation VIII Pokémon Brilliant Diamond and Shining Pearl Planted 0 hours Sprouted 18 hours Taller 36 hours Flowering 54 hours Berries 72 hours A Yache plant will yield 2-5 Berries.
